summary: Strong 1976 performance highlighted by an epic Scarlet Begonias and solid second‑set jams; audience tape is decent but a bit muddy at the start.
review: Fans rave about this October 1976 show – the 13‑minute “Scarlet Begonias” riff, a groovy “Sugaree,” and a jam‑filled second set featuring “The Wheel,” “Comes A Time,” and “Dancin’ In The Streets” are repeatedly called out as standout moments.  The recording is an audience tape: it captures the hall ambience and overall band balance well after the first couple of songs, though the opening is described as muddy or low‑level.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviewers praise the musical performance (many 4–5 star comments) while noting consistent but not fatal recording flaws; weighted scoring yields a solid >4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Muddy/low clarity at the very start, improves after first two songs; room ambience captured nicely, low crowd noise, overall decent balance for an audience tape.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid with a standout “Scarlet Begonias” and sweet “Loser”; second set launches into “Playing in the Band” and builds through jam‑heavy sequences, delivering an especially juicy, improvisational climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers highlight Jerry’s riff work and guitar tone as amazing, especially on Scarlet.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass lines are praised alongside Jerry’s riffs for blowing the audience away.
